steps.title: Identify what the blank needs to do
steps.content: The sentence describes films “by Indigenous women ______ featured in film festivals in recent years.” The blank introduces a verb phrase that modifies “films” — it must be an infinitive form that describes a completed past action relative to the present.

steps.title: Eliminate choices that can't function as infinitive modifiers
steps.content:
• Choice A — to have been: The perfect infinitive correctly expresses a completed action and can post-modify a noun phrase. ✓
  • Choice B — were being: A past-progressive finite verb; cannot serve as a noun modifier here. ✗
  • Choice C — had been: A finite past-perfect verb; cannot serve as an infinitive modifier in this position. ✗
  • Choice D — are being: A finite present-progressive verb; cannot serve as an infinitive modifier. ✗
A is correct.
